baseline.summary: Passionate Computer Science student with a strong foundation in mathematical and computational principles. Seeking a Quantitative Trading Internship at Castellan Capital to leverage my background in probability, statistics, linear algebra, and Python programming proficiency for robust systematic trading signal research and backtesting.

lines.text: Product & Design Intern at KuKClean (Bangalore, Jun–Jul 2024) — Utilized Python and Figma to produce creative assets and set up product listings on the e-commerce platform.

Quantitative Trading Intern at Castellan Capital (targeting Summer 2026) — Aspired role to develop systematic trading signals using Python, C++, NumPy, and internal backtesting frameworks. High-performance research environment is ideal for my expertise in probability, statistics, linear algebra, and machine learning.

Meridian Project — Designed a multi-agent system in Python with FastAPI, pulling data from yfinance, Wikipedia, Reddit, and Google News to generate due-diligence memos on NSE/BSE companies. Ensured correctness through a schema that links claims to evidence rows.

CrowdSense — Developed a real-time crowd-monitoring platform using CSRNet and YOLOv8 for density estimation, switching between models based on scene density. Implemented zone-wise risk detection with OpenCV optical flow for privacy-preserving movement analysis.

Anchor Project — Built an n8n-orchestrated job-pipeline system to create researched application packets from job postings, incorporating a Grounding Critic to ensure factual accuracy and full observability logging.
Programming proficiency in Python (NumPy, FastAPI, asyncio) and familiarity with C++ for quantitative research. Strong background in probability, statistics, linear algebra, and machine learning.
Experience with time-series analysis and backtesting frameworks. Proficiency in data structures, algorithms, and web development using React, Next.js, and Figma.
Pursuing a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science at RV College of Engineering (2024–2028), with a focus on machine learning and data science.
Completed Data Science for Engineers from IIT Madras (2026) and pursuing Machine Learning Specialization from Stanford Online + DeepLearning.AI.

cover_letter_paragraphs: Dear Hiring Manager at Castellan Capital,

I am excited to apply for the Quantitative Trading Internship in your Systematic Trading team. As a Computer Science student with a strong foundation in probability, statistics, and linear algebra, I am eager to leverage my skills in Python programming and systematic trading research within an environment that values high-performance and rigorous quantitative analysis.

My experience as a Product & Design Intern at KuKClean and the development of Meridian — a multi-agent system for generating due-diligence memos — have honed my ability to work on complex, data-driven projects with precise requirements. This aligns well with your need for robust systematic trading signal research and backtesting tools.

I am particularly drawn to Castellan Capital’s culture, which emphasizes high-performance, research-driven work. My background in probability, statistics, and machine learning, combined with my hands-on experience with Python and C++, positions me to contribute effectively to your team. I look forward to the opportunity to present my research findings and collaborate on strategies that could drive real-world impact.
Thank you for considering my application. I am excited about the prospect of joining Castellan Capital and contributing to your team's goals.

violations: Line 1 (from full-resume) claims 'utilized Python and Figma' — no mention of Figma in this line's Source.
Line 2 (from full-resume) claims 'high-performance research environment is ideal for my expertise' — no such claim or detail present in the paired Source.

Line 3 (from full-resume) claims 'designed a multi-agent system with FastAPI' — only FastAPI stack mentioned, not multi-agent system. Also, 'ensured correctness through a schema that links claims to evidence rows' is stronger and broader than what's supported by the paired Source.
